hero.title2: the way you want on GitHub
hero.subtitle: Recent activity can reshuffle your repository list and hide your best work. With GitPins, you decide which projects should appear first.

visualDemo.title: See the difference before you connect anything
visualDemo.subtitle: Same profile, same repositories, two completely different first impressions. GitPins lets you keep your showcase stable instead of letting the latest random commit decide it.
visualDemo.note: Visual simulation of the same GitHub profile before and after applying a saved GitPins order.

states.noisy: Recent activity pushes it to the top
states.hidden: Strong project, but now buried lower
states.showcase: Key project kept visible on purpose
states.stable: Important repo stays in the showcase
states.moved: Still there, but no longer steals the spotlight

before.label: Before
before.title: GitHub default order
before.desc: A quick commit to an old or secondary repo can move it above the projects you actually want people to see first.

after.label: After
after.title: GitPins curated order
after.desc: Your key repositories are prioritized in the public list. A later sync can restore the order after new activity changes it.

step1.title: Authorize and install
step1.desc: Sign in with GitHub and install the app only on the repositories you want to manage

step2.title: Filter and order
step2.desc: Search, filter, and drag up to 100 repositories into your priority list

step3.title: Save and sync
step3.desc: Save your configuration and apply the order manually from the dashboard

step4.title: Schedule if needed
step4.desc: For ongoing automation, connect GitHub Actions, cron, or another external scheduler

q1.question: Does it modify my code?
q1.answer: The current strategy creates no commits, branches, or file changes. GitPins uses Contents: write exclusively to create and delete a temporary tag ref during sync.

q2.question: How much does it cost?
q2.answer: GitPins is free and open source. Manual sync does not use GitHub Actions; if you choose Actions as your scheduler, it will use the corresponding minutes from your account.

q3.question: Can I stop using it?
q3.answer: Yes. Disable your scheduler, uninstall the GitHub App, and optionally delete your account and data from Settings. You can also self-host the project.

q4.question: Does it work with private repos?
q4.answer: Yes, provided you authorize access and install the GitHub App on those repositories. You can include, exclude, or filter them in the dashboard.

q5.question: Is it safe for my repositories?
q5.answer: GitPins does not request permission to delete repositories or read Actions secrets. Contents: write is required to manage refs; the current open-source code limits this to temporary tags.

q6.question: What happens if I uninstall GitPins?
q6.answer: No repositories or files are deleted. The resulting order remains until new GitHub activity changes it. Also disable any external scheduler to avoid failed calls.

noLimit.title: Up to 100 priorities
noLimit.desc: Beyond GitHub's six native pinned repositories, prioritize up to 100 projects in your repository list.

automatic.title: Manual or scheduled
automatic.desc: Run sync from the dashboard or connect GitHub Actions, cron, or any HTTP scheduler.

noLockIn.title: No vendor lock-in
noLockIn.desc: The code, API, and documentation are public. Audit it or deploy your own instance.

cleanCommits.title: No commits or branches
cleanCommits.desc: Temporary tags are removed after each touch. GitPins adds no commits and creates no branches to order repos.

interviews.title: Job interviews
interviews.desc: Control what recruiters see first on your profile. Show your best projects, not that last commit to a test repo.

developers.title: Developers
developers.desc: Your GitHub profile is your portfolio. Make sure your most relevant projects are always visible.

companies.title: Companies
companies.desc: Let clients see your flagship projects, not dependabot updates.

openSource.title: Open Source
openSource.desc: Highlight your main libraries so contributors can find them easily.

disclaimer.text2: By continuing, you accept that you use this service at your own risk. The code is open source and available for you to review, improve, or run on your own infrastructure.

welcome.title: Welcome to GitPins
welcome.desc: This guide shows you how to order repos, save changes, sync, and understand the automation options.

headerControls.title: Quick controls

poolZone.title: Repository pool

deleteModal.title: Delete account
deleteModal.intro: This action is irreversible. Make sure you download your data first if you need it.
deleteModal.item1: Will be deleted: profile, settings, tokens, snapshots and logs.
deleteModal.item2: Will not be deleted: any GitHub repositories or code.
deleteModal.item3: If you set up a scheduled workflow (for example in a repo named gitpins-config), disable or delete it to stop scheduled sync.
deleteModal.reauthDesc: To protect your account, GitPins requires a recent reauthentication before deleting data.

strategyInfo.title: How ordering is applied
strategyInfo.intro: To keep your repos on top, GitPins updates repository recency by creating a temporary tag ref that points to HEAD and removing it immediately.

revert.title: Temporary ref touch
revert.desc: Creates a temporary tag ref that points to HEAD, then deletes it immediately. Your default branch history stays clean.

strategyInfo.note: GitPins creates no commits, branches, or file changes. It only creates and deletes temporary tags.

howItWorksPage.title: How GitPins works
howItWorksPage.subtitle: Technical explanation of how we keep your repositories ordered

problem.title: The problem
problem.p1: GitHub orders repositories on your profile by last update date. This means that every time you push to a repo, it goes to the top of your list.
problem.p2: If you have important projects you want to show first, but you work on other repositories, your highlighted projects get buried.

solution.title: The solution
solution.p1: GitPins uses a simple mechanism: update repository recency signals in the order you choose.
solution.p2: Temporary tags point to the existing HEAD and are deleted immediately. No commits, branches, or file changes are created.

step1.title: Configure your order
step1.desc: In the GitPins dashboard, you drag and drop your repositories in the order you want. You can choose to order all your repos or just the first N.

step2.title: Install the GitHub App
step2.desc: Install the GitHub App on the repositories you want GitPins to manage. This allows GitPins to create/delete temporary refs (no file changes) when syncing.

step3.title: Scheduled sync (optional)
step3.desc: If you want GitPins to keep your order automatically, run a scheduled workflow (GitHub Actions or any scheduler) that calls your GitPins instance.

step4.title: Reorders if necessary
step4.desc: When sync runs, GitPins checks GitHub's current order and only touches the repos needed to restore your desired top list.

strategies.title: Ordering strategy
strategies.intro: GitPins uses a single, predictable strategy to keep your history auditable:

revert.title: Temporary ref touch
revert.desc: We create a temporary tag ref that points to HEAD and delete it immediately. Recency updates without adding commits to your default branch.

actionCode.title: The external scheduler
actionCode.intro: GitPins does not run cron itself. GitHub Actions is a common option, but you can also use cron or any scheduler capable of calling your instance over HTTP.
actionCode.whatItDoes: What happens during a scheduled run?

steps.step1: Calls your GitPins instance (/api/sync) with your per-user sync secret
steps.step2: GitPins validates settings, UTC hour, cooldown, and current order:
steps.step2a: Calculates the minimum prefix that must be touched
steps.step2b: Resolves the current SHA of the default branch
steps.step2c: Creates and deletes a temporary tag ref for that SHA
steps.step3: Runs a single reverse-order pass and rate-limits API calls
steps.step4: Stores results and duration in GitPins logs

actionCode.codeLocation: If you use GitHub Actions for scheduled sync, the workflow lives in .github/workflows/maintain-order.yml inside the repository you chose for it.

security.title: Security and permissions
security.permissionsTitle: Exact permissions of our GitHub App
security.permissionsIntro: Our application requests the minimum necessary permissions. This is exactly what we can and CANNOT do:

canDo.title: What GitPins does:
canDo.item1: Read profile and authorized repository metadata
canDo.item2: Resolve HEAD and create temporary tags
canDo.item3: Delete each temporary tag after applying the touch
canDo.item4: Store settings, history, and sync logs

cannotDo.title: What GitPins does not do:
cannotDo.item1: Does not delete repositories - That permission is not requested
cannotDo.item2: Does not create commits, branches, or file changes
cannotDo.item3: Does not read Actions secrets or private variables
cannotDo.item5: Does not access Issues, PRs, Wikis, or Discussions
cannotDo.item6: Does not invite collaborators or change permissions

security.technicalNote: Technical note: Contents: write technically provides write capability, but the current code limits it to temporary tags. Review installed permissions from

noCodeStorage.title: We don't store your code
noCodeStorage.desc: We only store ordering settings and logs in our database. We do not clone repositories or store any code.

runsOnYourAccount.title: The scheduler is under your control
runsOnYourAccount.desc: If you choose GitHub Actions, the workflow runs in your repository. You can also use cron or another scheduler.

canDisable.title: You can disable it anytime
canDisable.desc: Disable automatic sync in Settings and pause the external scheduler. Your saved order remains.

openSource.title: Open source and auditable
openSource.desc: The application, endpoint, and workflow example are public and can be self-hosted.

affectsCode.a: No. Temporary refs do not modify tracked files and are deleted automatically.

actionsMinutes.q: How many Actions minutes does it use?
actionsMinutes.a: Manual sync uses no Actions minutes. If you use GitHub Actions as a scheduler, consumption depends on your configured frequency and the current rules of your plan.

privateRepos.q: Does it work with private repositories?
privateRepos.a: Yes, if you authorize access and install the GitHub App on those repositories. Include or exclude them in Settings.

whatIfPush.q: What if I push to a repo?
whatIfPush.a: Activity can change recency. Sync manually or let your external scheduler call GitPins again to restore the order.

canPause.a: Yes. In settings you can disable automatic sync without losing your configuration. You can also disable the workflow directly on GitHub.

howItWorksPage.footer: GitPins - Control the order of your repositories

disclaimer.title: Disclaimer
disclaimer.p1: GitPins is an open source project provided "AS IS" and "AS AVAILABLE", without warranties of any kind, whether express or implied, including, but not limited to, the implied warranties of merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose, and non-infringement.
disclaimer.p2: In no event shall the authors, contributors, or copyright holders be liable for any claim, damages, or other liability, whether in an action of contract, tort, or otherwise, arising from, out of, or in connection with the software or the use or other dealings in the software.
disclaimer.p3: By using GitPins, you accept that you do so at your own responsibility and risk.

howItWorks.title: How GitPins Works - Full Transparency
howItWorks.intro: GitPins modifies repository recency through temporary refs. It's important that you understand exactly what it does:

step1.title: Temporary Tag Ref
step1.desc: GitPins creates a short-lived tag ref pointing to the current HEAD commit.

step2.title: Temporary Ref Touch
step2.desc: The temporary tag ref is deleted immediately. This updates recency without adding commits to your default branch.

step3.title: GitHub Actions
step3.desc: The entire process runs via a GitHub Action in your own account. The Action runs with your credentials and permissions.

risks.title: Potential Risks
risks.intro: Although GitPins is designed to be safe, there are potential risks you should know about:

risk1.title: Git History
risk1.desc: Temporary refs can appear briefly during sync. They are removed automatically and default branch history remains clean.

risk2.title: API Dependency
risk2.desc: GitPins depends on the GitHub API. Changes to the API could affect the service's functionality.

risk3.title: Permissions
risk3.desc: The GitHub App requires write permissions on your repositories to create/delete temporary refs. Review the permissions carefully.

risk4.title: Actions Minutes
risk4.desc: The GitHub Action consumes minutes from your account. Private repos consume double the minutes.

noDelete.title: GitPins CANNOT Delete Your Repositories
noDelete.p1: It's important to clarify that GitPins does NOT have permissions to delete repositories. The GitHub App requests only the minimum necessary permissions:
noDelete.canDo2: Create and delete temporary refs (without modifying files)
noDelete.canDo3: Create and delete temporary refs to apply ordering touches
noDelete.canDo4: Provide a sync endpoint that a scheduler (like GitHub Actions) can call
noDelete.cannotDo1: Delete repositories - We don't have that permission
noDelete.cannotDo5: Access Issues, Pull Requests, or Wikis
noDelete.verify: You can verify the exact permissions at any time from

privacy.title: Privacy & Data
privacy.intro: GitPins stores only the data needed to run the service. You control your data from Settings at any time.
privacy.store1: Account metadata (GitHub id, username, optional email and avatar)
privacy.store2: Ordering settings (pinned list, limits, scheduling, and sync configuration)
privacy.store3: Operational history (order snapshots and sync logs) to help you understand changes and debug issues
privacy.store4: Security audit events (pseudonymized) to prevent abuse and investigate incidents
privacy.tokensP1: OAuth tokens are encrypted at rest. We never include tokens or sync secrets in exports.
privacy.tokensP2: Encryption details: AES-256-GCM authenticated encryption. Each token is encrypted with a unique random salt and IV.
privacy.tokensP3: Token expiry: GitHub can issue expiring access tokens. GitPins stores the expiration timestamp and refreshes automatically when possible. If a token cannot be refreshed, you will be asked to log in again.
privacy.exportP1: You can download a JSON export from Settings. Exports expire after 72 hours.
privacy.deleteP1: You can delete your GitPins account from Settings. Deletion requires a recent GitHub reauthentication (sudo mode).
privacy.deleteP2: Deleting your GitPins account does not delete any GitHub repositories. If you set up a scheduled workflow (for example in `gitpins-config`), disable or delete it to stop scheduled sync.

yourResponsibility.title: Your Responsibility
yourResponsibility.p1: By using GitPins, you accept that you have read and understood how the service works. You understand that temporary refs are created and removed as part of the ordering process.
yourResponsibility.p2: You accept the potential risks described on this page and are responsible for monitoring GitHub Actions minutes usage on your account.
yourResponsibility.p3: You can disable the service at any time by disabling/deleting any scheduled workflow you configured, without any penalty.

noWarranty.title: No Warranties
noWarranty.p1: GitPins is provided without warranty of any kind. We do not guarantee that the service will be available without interruption or that it will be free of errors.
noWarranty.p2: Results may not be accurate or reliable in all cases, and the service may not meet your specific requirements. Use it at your own risk.

limitation.title: Limitation of Liability
limitation.p1: To the maximum extent permitted by applicable law, in no event shall the creators, contributors, or maintainers of GitPins be liable for any direct, indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages, including, without limitation:
limitation.item5: Any other pecuniary or non-pecuniary damages
limitation.p2: This limitation applies regardless of whether the damage arises from:
limitation.item2_1: Use or inability to use the service
limitation.item2_3: Errors, viruses, or bugs in the software
limitation.item2_4: Any other circumstance related to the service

openSource.title: Open Source
openSource.p1: GitPins is open source software under the MIT license. You can:
openSource.item4: Modify the code for your needs

contact.title: Contact
contact.p1: To report security issues, bugs, or legal questions, open an issue on our GitHub repository:

acceptance.title: Acceptance of Terms
acceptance.p1: By using GitPins, you confirm that you have read, understood, and accepted all the terms of this disclaimer.

banned.title: Account Suspended
banned.message: Your account has been suspended due to a violation of our terms of service. If you believe this is a mistake, please contact the administrator.
banned.contact: To appeal this decision or get more information, please open an issue on our GitHub repository:

install.title: Install GitPins on GitHub
install.subtitle: To order your repositories, GitPins needs to be installed as a GitHub App on the repos you want to manage.

steps.step1: Choose the repositories you want GitPins to manage, or install it on all repositories.
steps.step2: GitPins only needs permission to create and delete temporary refs. It does not edit tracked files.
steps.step3: You can uninstall the app at any time directly from GitHub.

install.can1: Create temporary refs on the repositories you select.
install.can2: Restore the order you configured in GitPins.
install.can3: Be removed at any time without affecting your repositories.
install.cannot2: Modify your tracked files or push code changes.
install.cannot3: Read more than the permissions granted by the GitHub App.
install.redirectNote: You will be redirected to GitHub to complete the installation.

admin.title: GitPins Admin

accessDenied.title: 403 - Admin access required
accessDenied.desc: Your account is authenticated, but it is not in the admin allowlist.

banModal.title: Ban @{username}
banModal.message: This user will not be able to log in or use GitPins until unbanned.

admin.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete user @{username}? This action cannot be undone.

errors.reauthRequired: Recent reauthentication required. Redirecting to GitHub...

hero.title: Help Center
hero.subtitle: How to configure, apply, and maintain repository order while keeping control of permissions and data

step1.title: Authorize and install
step1.desc: Sign in and install the GitHub App on the repos you want to manage

step2.title: Filter and order
step2.desc: Search, filter, and drag repositories into the section you want to keep on top

step3.title: Save
step3.desc: Save the order and settings to create a restorable snapshot

step4.title: Apply
step4.desc: Sync manually or connect an external scheduler for automation

githubApp.title: GitHub authorization and App installation
githubApp.intro: GitPins combines user authorization, used to identify you and display your repositories, with a GitHub App installation that performs ordering operations only on authorized repositories.
githubApp.permUser: Identify your account, read email when available, and retrieve the repository list you can access
githubApp.permMetadata: Read name, description, visibility, language, stars, and default branch
githubApp.permContents: Resolve HEAD and exclusively create or delete the temporary tags used for ordering
githubApp.can1: Read profile and authorized repository metadata
githubApp.can2: Create and delete a temporary tag ref during each touch
githubApp.can3: Store your order, settings, snapshots, and sync logs
githubApp.can4: Export or delete your account data when requested
githubApp.cannot2: Create commits, create branches, or edit files in the current strategy
githubApp.cannot3: Read GitHub Actions secrets or private repository variables
githubApp.cannot4: Operate on repositories outside the authorized installation
githubApp.permissionNote: Contents: write technically provides write capability. The current code limits its use to creating and deleting temporary tags, so this section describes actual GitPins behavior rather than an absolute limitation of the permission.
githubApp.verify: You can verify and revoke permissions at any time from

ordering.title: Order repositories
ordering.intro: The dashboard separates repositories you want to keep on top from the rest. Search and filter by public/private visibility, language, owner, and stars before dragging them.
ordering.demoDesc: Click a repository to add it to the priority section. Use the X to remove it.
ordering.save1: Your order is stored in the database
ordering.save2: One of the latest 20 restorable snapshots is created
ordering.save3: Saving does not change GitHub: click Sync now to apply the order
ordering.save4: Automation is optional and requires an external scheduler

sync.title: Manual and scheduled sync
sync.intro: Saving defines the desired order. Sync compares it with GitHub and touches only the repositories needed to restore it.
sync.manualDesc: The Sync now button calls an authenticated, CSRF-protected endpoint. Runs that are too close together are skipped during a 10-minute cooldown.
sync.scheduledDesc: GitPins exposes POST /api/sync but does not run cron itself. GitHub Actions, cron, or another scheduler must make the request.
sync.frequencyReality: Actual frequency is controlled by the external cron. The value stored in Settings does not automatically modify an existing workflow.
sync.hourReality: Preferred hour is a UTC rule: scheduled calls outside that hour are skipped. The scheduler must call during the selected hour.
sync.actionDesc: Create this workflow in a repository you control. Define GITPINS_APP_URL as a variable and GITPINS_SYNC_SECRET as a repository secret.
sync.secretNote: Never write the sync secret in YAML or publish it. Any scheduler capable of sending the same POST request can be used instead.

strategies.title: How GitPins applies the order
strategies.intro: GitPins uses one strategy: a temporary tag-ref touch. There is no alternative branch-based or commit-based strategy.
strategies.step1: Resolve HEAD of the default branch
strategies.revertPro3: A single pass processed in reverse order
strategies.revertPro4: Touches only the minimum required prefix

history.title: History and activity export
history.intro: The dashboard combines order snapshots and sync logs. Restorable snapshots are limited to the latest 20.
history.exportDesc: The activity section exports entries you have loaded:
history.exportNote: The complete account export is a separate JSON download available in Settings.
history.restoreDesc: Restore any retained snapshot and apply it again with Sync now.
history.restoreNote: Restoring changes the saved configuration; GitHub is not modified until you run a sync.

privacy.title: Privacy and account control
privacy.intro: From Settings you can download stored data or delete your account. These actions affect GitPins and never delete GitHub repositories.
privacy.exportDesc: Generates JSON containing profile, settings, snapshots, logs, and available audit data. The file is prepared as a temporary job.
privacy.secretsDesc: OAuth tokens, refresh tokens, and the sync secret are never included in downloadable exports.
privacy.deleteDesc: Requires recent reauthentication and typed confirmation. It deletes GitPins user data, not repositories or code.

q1.a: Verify that the GitHub App can access the repos you want to order. Go to github.com/settings/installations, find GitPins, and check repository access.

q2.a: For manual sync, check installation, cooldown, and permissions. For scheduled sync, also verify the scheduler sends POST /api/sync with X-GitPins-Sync-Secret, automatic sync is enabled, and the call occurs during the selected UTC hour.

q3.a: Disable automatic sync in Settings and pause the external scheduler as well. Your saved order is retained.

q4.a: Disable any scheduler, uninstall the app at github.com/settings/installations, and delete your account in Settings if you also want stored data removed. No repository is deleted.

q5.q: GitPins commits appear in my history
q5.a: The current version creates no commits or branches. [GitPins] commits come from older versions. Removing them requires a history rewrite: create a backup and follow a reviewed manual procedure before force-pushing; GitPins does not run it automatically from the dashboard.

q6.a: This is expected after commits, merges, releases, or other activity. Run Sync now or configure an external scheduler to periodically restore your priority list.

cta.title: Have more questions?
cta.subtitle: Check the dashboard or open an issue on GitHub
